title: Rank-Preserving OOF Blend With Sigmoid Calibration Only On The Final Mix
summary: AUC is rank-based, so isotonic calibration can hurt by introducing ties, while sigmoid calibration preserves ranking.
rationale: AUC is rank-based, so isotonic calibration can hurt by introducing ties, while sigmoid calibration preserves ranking. That makes a good testable direction a specialist blend first, then apply only a monotonic calibrator to the blended score if public submissions benefit.
implementation_hint: Build 2-3 specialists with different inductive biases, such as current feature-rich GBDT/AutoML, CatBoost categorical specialist, and hazard-style model. Generate strict out-of-fold predictions, combine them with a simple linear or logistic meta-blend, and if needed calibrate only the final blend with `method='sigmoid'` on OOF predictions. Compare raw blend vs sigmoid-calibrated blend on CV and leaderboard.
expected_effect: Can capture complementary ranking signal while avoiding the public-score degradation that often comes from overfitted non-monotonic calibration.
risk: If specialists are too correlated, the blend adds complexity without lift; calibration done on in-fold predictions will leak badly.
